name: Muramasa Blades
description: The Muramasa Blades are a collection of swords forged by the legendary swordsmith Muramasa Sengo in feudal Japan. Muramasa was known for his exceptional skill but also for his volatile and potentially cursed nature. Legend states that his blades were imbued with his own violent spirit, making them incredibly sharp and powerful but also prone to driving their wielders to madness and bloodshed. They are believed to crave blood and will not be satisfied until they have tasted it, often leading their users to unnecessary violence. The swords are said to be cursed, bringing misfortune and tragedy to those who possess them. In contrast to the swords of his rival, Masamune, which were said to be imbued with peace and justice, Muramasa's blades represented destruction and chaos. The association with violence and the potential for corruption led to many samurai and warlords avoiding Muramasa blades, especially during times of peace. The Tokugawa shogunate even banned their possession, further cementing their reputation as dangerous and cursed weapons. Despite the negative connotations, the Muramasa blades remain a powerful symbol of skill, danger, and the duality of power in Japanese folklore. Their legacy continues to inspire stories and legends to this day.
subtitle: Cursed blades of unparalleled sharpness.
origin: Forged by Muramasa Sengo, imbued with his violent spirit.
currentLocation: Unknown, scattered in museums and private collections.
associatedHeroes: Tokugawa Ieyasu (indirectly, due to the ban)

keyMyths.title: The Bloodthirsty Blade
keyMyths.description: A Muramasa blade is said to crave blood, driving its wielder to commit acts of violence even when unnecessary. The blade won't rest until it is satisfied.

keyMyths.title: Tokugawa's Curse
keyMyths.description: Tokugawa Ieyasu's family suffered misfortunes attributed to Muramasa blades, leading to a ban on their possession. This further cemented the blade's reputation as cursed.

sources.author: Kanzan Sato
sources.description: Provides historical context on Japanese swordsmiths.
sources.text: Provides historical context on Japanese swordsmiths.

sources.author: Stephen Turnbull
